semantic.role: CLI command adapter for skill management operations (install, uninstall, list). It is a thin controller that parses CLI arguments, displays help/usage, and delegates to the skill service layer.
responsibilities: Parse the first CLI argument as a subcommand (install, uninstall, list, etc.).
Invoke `installSkill` from the skill service with appropriate arguments for the 'install' subcommand.
Invoke `uninstallSkill` from the skill service with appropriate arguments for the 'uninstall' subcommand.
Display a list of installed skills with their descriptions for the 'list' subcommand.
Display usage help and exit with error code when an unknown subcommand is provided.
Provide the typed interface `ExecuteSkillCommandOptions` for the command options contract.
outOfScope: Skill installation logic – handled by skill-service.
Skill uninstallation logic – handled by skill-service.
Any file system or network operations – delegated to service layer.
Pipeline or persistence logic – this module is purely an adapter.
invariants: The CLI entrypoint must not contain business logic or persistence logic; this file adheres by delegating entirely to skill-service.
The subcommand must be the first element of the args array.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide a dedicated CLI command for managing ArchSpine skills (installing, uninstalling, listing) that keeps the command layer thin and delegates to a service.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: No recent changes detected in this file from the git intent (the intent mentioned removing legacy CLI aliases and rebuilding docs, but this file may not have been directly affected).

publicSurface.description: Top-level CLI handler for the 'skill' command. Parses subcommands and delegates to the skill service layer. Called by the CLI router when the user runs `spine skill <subcommand>`.
Options interface specifying the command-line arguments (array of strings) to be passed to executeSkillCommand.
